q: In some examination, A scored 50 marks while B scored 75 marks, whereas the maximum marks is 110. By how many percent (of total marks) is B ahead of A? (Round off the answer to two decimal places.)
The average age of 23 students in a class is 25 years. If the teacher's age is also included, the average age of all of them becomes 26 years. What is the teacher's age (in years)?
If a pole is 30 m high and forms a shadow of 10√3 m length on the ground, then what will be the angle of elevation of the sun?
If 4^(x+1) = 64 × 8^x, then find the value of x.

Two persons together can finish a piece of work in 5 hours; the same work can be finished by the first person alone in 6 days. If both work on the same day, how much time will they take to complete the work?

A shopkeeper sells two articles together for Rs 80. He sells the first article at a profit of 1 rupee and the second article at a profit of 2 rupees, and its cost price is Rs 70. What is the cost price of the first article?
A typist takes 40 minutes to type 4 pages. In 90 minutes how many pages will he type?
The age of a person is 5 times the age of his brother. But the person is 27 years older than his brother in the present. What is the brother's age (in years)?

Rs 1,964 is divided among a man, a woman and a boy in such a way that if the man's share, the woman's share and the boy's share are taken, the man's share is in the ratio 20.4 : 13 to the woman's share. Find the boy's share.
If 10% of 1/3 of a number is 40%, find the number 477.
If a girl's height is 19.8 more than her friend's height, then her height is _____ more than her friend's. (Use a proportionality formula to find your answer.)
The diameter of a metal ball is 408 times the diameter of another, made by melting it down. If both the spherical balls were placed in a row, then find the number of balls (made).
On a track, one cyclist covers 420 m in 140 seconds. The second cyclist covers the same in 70 seconds. Find the average speed (in m/sec) of the cyclist.
Vishala walks at her normal speed of 3/5 of her speed and reaches her workplace 24 minutes late. Normally, how much time does she take to reach from her home to her workplace?
Find the compound interest obtained on a principal of Rs 1,500 at an annual compound interest rate of 10% (compounded annually) in 2 years.
A person sells one box at a profit of 7%. If he had sold it for Rs 1,280 more, he would have got a profit of 17%. Find the cost price of the box.
In the equation X/125 = 5/X, if X > 0, then what will come in place of 'x'?
A trader sells his goods at a 44% lower purchase price than the marked price; if he gives a 73% discount on the marked price, then what is his loss percent?

The price of an item increases by 40%, 15% and 45% over three consecutive months, but after that the price decreases by 70%. By what percentage did the price of the item increase compared to its original price? (Round off your answer to two decimal places.)

A rectangular base room is 10 ft high and its floor area is 25,000 ft². If all four walls of the room are painted at 25 paise per ft², what amount will be spent to paint the four walls and the ceiling of the room?

A trader offers the following schemes: A) 32% and 40% successive discount; B) 1 quarter, 5 quarters discount; C) 48% and one successive discount; D) 8% and 33% successive discount. Which one of the following schemes is most beneficial for the trader?
The ratio of two numbers is 3 : 4 and their HCF (highest common factor) is 7. Find their LCM (lowest common multiple).

In a class, all 23 students are standing in a line in a particular direction facing each other; from one end the position of a student is 8th place and from the other end his place is 16th. How many students stand between them?
Select the option that follows the same pattern as that between the given pair of letter-clusters. Both pairs follow the same pattern. VYA-EHJ, HKM-QTV

Given statements and conclusions, you have to assume that the things stated in the statements are true even if they seem to differ from commonly known facts. Decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the statements. Statements: (i) Some elephants are dogs. (ii) Some dogs are cats. Conclusions: (I) Some elephants are cats. (II) Some cats are dogs. Which conclusion follows?
If '+' and '-' are interchanged with each other and also '×' and '÷' are interchanged, then what will come in place of '?' in the following equation? 79 + 7 + 464 × 4 - 386 = ?

Seven persons A, B, C, D, E, F and G are sitting in a row, facing north. D is sitting between A and B. Three persons sit between B and E. F is sitting just to the right of E. G is sitting just to the left of A. A is the neighbour of B. How many persons sit to the right of C?

Eight boxes A, B, C, D, E, F and G, are kept one above another, but not necessarily in the same order. A is kept just above E. E is kept above C. B is kept just above D. Between A and B, only one box is kept. How many boxes are kept between A and B?
What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 170 150 133 119 108 ?

MGRC, HCOA are related in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, VOWB, QKTZ are related. If this same pattern is followed, then which of the following options is related to QNVT in the same way?

In a certain code language: A + B means that 'A is the son of B', A - B means 'A is the wife of B', A × B means 'A is the brother of B', and A & B means 'A is the father of B'. If 'K & T × R - Z + V' is true, then how is V related to K?
o: V is the father of K's wife
V is the father of K
V is the mother of K's wife

q: What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 6 10 18 30 46 ?

A, B, C, D, E, F and G are sitting around a circular table, facing the centre. E is sitting just to the right of B. B is to the immediate right of A. D, F are sitting just to the left of A. G is sitting just to the right of A. How many persons sit between C and G?
What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 4 9 14 19 24 ?

Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as the numbers of the given set are related. (Note: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers without breaking them into their constituent digits; e.g. 13 — performing operations like adding, subtracting, multiplying etc. on 13 is allowed, but breaking it into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on these is not allowed.) (15, 39, 87) (27, 51, 99)

Select the option that correctly arranges the given number series. (All the numbers are single-digit numbers. Counting is to be done only from the right side.) 6 3 4 1 9 5 1 3 6 5 3 2 7 4 9 8 4 2 7 8 4 2 5 7 6 4 5 (DTI). Among such numbers, the first number before each is odd and the number just after it is even.

This question is based on the three-digit numbers given below. (469) 458 625 751 359 245 (DTI). (To the leftmost digit add 6, to the middle digit add 9 and to the third digit add 7.) (Note: All operations are to be performed on the given numbers.) If the numbers are arranged in descending order, then how many numbers' positions will remain unchanged?
In a certain code language, 'trust builds bond' is coded as 'zn fy tk' and 'keep your trust' is coded as 'fy ep rb'. In that language, how is 'trust' coded?
In a certain code language, 'FOUR' is coded as '5812' and 'ROUT' is coded as '5628'. In that language, what is the code for 'T'?

A point starts moving from point B and goes towards the north in direction for 37 km. Then it turns right and goes 40 km. Then it turns right again and travels 55 km. Then it again turns right and goes 18 km. Then it turns right and travels 64 km. The point reaches point A. In which direction and how far is point A from the starting point B (the midpoint of the 90-degree turns)?

Given statements and conclusions, you have to assume that the things stated in the statements are true even if they seem to differ from commonly known facts. Decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the statements. Statements: (i) All players are guitarists. (ii) Some guitarists are singers. Conclusions: (I) Some singers are guitarists. (II) Some singers are players. Which conclusion follows?
